author: claude
content: STORAGE DECISION (Stefan 2026-06-20): go DIRECT-to-public-Postgres (NOT edge-buffered split-sink). Trust model — volunteers are close friends, so the abuse/quota/credential-isolation hardening concern is WAIVED. Remaining technical (NOT solved by trust): (1) write-reliability of a direct DB connection over flaky home networks, (2) the unstructured-data-no-binds problem (response bodies must go to Postgres bytea / S3 / or be disabled). Relatedly, Stefan flagged a real forward-progress bug in the OLD parquet->S3 caching path (sites perpetually marked in-progress by the WQ but never committed/pushed) — investigating the StorageController's liveness guarantees separately.
